author: @CGrenfeldt
text:
This should  be able to used as a clutch!
Using simple disc breaks instead if normal clutches.

Engine to center gear, put a break on the big wheel or combined planet package and let the other one go to the gearbox.

Same principal can be used to make cheap dynamic 4wheels drive.
Freely spinning and all power go to front wheels. When engaged by "breaking" then power go to all wheels.

If both front and back have this box then got can choose if u want power in only front or backwheels. Or 4-wheel.

author: @CGrenfeldt
text:
This is very interesting.
What if your put three weak engines on each planetary wheels.
They should all be sumorized.

Would three weak wind or water turbines be added into one force that's more efficient than harvest three separate?

replies.author: @CGrenfeldt
replies.text:
And then u stack three of these units as input to another planetary gear.

When I was thinking about building my own electric scooter i was thinking about using planetary gears to make a compact engine of three 'weaker' and cheaper engines.
And then use the outer ring to drive the wheel. Or another set of gears to get one to one-isch ratio again.

Three engines gives you the option to only run one or two to cruse

author: @maquabear5242
text: It seems to me that you may have the right idea about using these things for using big wide veined windmills to generate electricity. Since they turn slowly, but have a great amount of torque. Translating the massive torque from the outer gear to the inner sun gear would spin it fast enough to generate electricity.

author: @maquabear5242
text: The first time that I became aware of planetary gears, was when I was operating heavy equipment. The mechanic was called to work on my TS 14-B Terrex earth mover, Scraper. He took off the wheel and low and behold there were huge planetary gears.

author: @Moist_yet_Crispy
text:
Goddess, I love this channel.
In terms of the potential bonding issue you talked about around 8:13 I feel like a JB weld would work great. A bit expensive tho. I know you have some great information out there in your books about bio glues and stuff like that. If you're up for it I'd love to see some experimentation with JB weld for these gears VS whatever you can come up with for glues. You know how to make things accessible for tinkerers like me and a good JB weld substitute would be absolutely amazing!  I haven't a clue as to how to give enough wear on those gears to make the test not take forever but I had the idea and thought it worth dropping a line here. I only briefly went through your book on bioplastics and need to dive in more. Sending love from AZ

author: @kellyfermon42
text: I  do not know if you have ever tore into a older style washing machine transmission. The first one that i did impressed me with all the bits and parts that were in it. If you never did have a look inside one you should give it a go. It was very fun for me and i did make a few things from all the cool bits.  Thanks for the Planetary Gears it has given me some ideas as a lot of your watching dose. It is amazing what you come up with.

author: @markmetzger5430
text: Polyurethane glue may have an advantage in this application. Sticks to virtually everything, catalyzed with water for a reasonably quick bond.The resulting bond is somewhat elastomeric/resilient  in nature. Fantastic videos Rob.

author: @lessweet3093
text: Would you do a video on what is the best type of lubricate to use on plastic and/or nylon(gears belts etc.)? I've heard some can break down the material. I've heard of people using old soap bars to prevent fan belts from being noisy...but idk if that' the same.

replies.author: @nil5221
replies.text: Someone mentioned first giving the belts a coat of Armour-all car vinyl protection. It makes  rubber so slippery. Then silicone grease which does not degrade the rubber like mineral grease does.

author: @kreynolds1123
text:
Rob, that's a beautiful and reasonable cheap planetary gear set.

Anyone/everyone else,  with wind power generation, sufficient RPM to generate sufficent voltage can be a problem with many generators. by fixing the blades to the ring gear with the planetary gears fix and the generator connected to the sun gear  would gear up the RPMs at the generator.

author: @ProlificInvention
text: Nope! The Nuvinci transmission is the ultimate design. Scalable, simple, near frictionless and the future of transportation in my opinion.

replies.author: @johnmcfadden9336
replies.text: I don’t think they would be as easy to replicate in a lot of circumstances but they are impressive thanks for making me aware of them

replies.author: @ProlificInvention
replies.text: @johnmcfadden9336 They are beyond simple to replicate at all scales honestly, other than that I agree!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it is very clever mate and I like it - but it cannot be frictionless - if it were frictionless there would be no power transfer - the spheres would just slip over the disks - the high pressure at the sphere disk interface compresses the transmission fluid to increase friction at that point so it acts like a tooth of a cog - I have no idea why folks are so hung ups on frictionless - once again there can be no power transfer without friction

replies.author: @ProlificInvention
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Near frictionless was the term I used to get yours and others attention.  It's simple,I be tested it on motorized bicycles and the amazing durability is only one characteristic that sets it apart from clutch/transmission combinations.  I have tested it and know for a fact that it's the scalable technology of the future.  Just my opinion obviously.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: @ProlificInvention like I say mate I do like it - I think it is very clever and reminds me very much of ferrofluid transmissions - but I also think  it is a genuine innovation
